artPrompt: A gaunt winged demon bureaucrat descends from a blood-dark sky on tattered black pinions, one taloned deathtouch hand extended palm-up demanding its due, hollow gold eyes patient and certain. A blank ledger dangles from its belt, a clock tower looms behind. The gesture is inevitable, predatory calm. Wide landscape, bone white and cold gold washes bleeding into black. Rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: The Tax Collector of Armageddon
text:
Flying, deathtouch
Whenever an opponent draws a card outside their draw step, they pay {1} or you put a doom counter on the Doomsday Clock and they lose 1 life. The end is coming; he still wants his cut.
{2}{B}, Remove four doom counters from the Doomsday Clock: Each opponent discards their hand and loses 4 life. Account settled.
flavorText: "There are two certainties. I am both of them."
